cases.title: Clean curved render
cases.description: Observer-facing curved film render with no storytelling overlays.
cases.communicates: Baseline perception. This is the curved wormhole view before interpretive scaffolding is added.

cases.title: Reference Reality / straight transport view
cases.description: Curved main render with the straight-path Reference Reality inset frozen for side-by-side comparison.
cases.communicates: Perceptual contrast. It shows what the scene would look like under straight transport and exposes the wormhole distortion as a difference against the main view.

cases.title: Curvature heat map only
cases.description: Fullscreen pass-1 curvature heat map using cumulative absolute turn angle, without semantic or collision overlays.
cases.communicates: Primary transport interpretation. It highlights where rays accumulate bending, without additional annotation competing for attention.

cases.title: Curvature plus semantic glyphs
cases.description: Curvature heat map combined with semantic field / BLV / portal glyph overlays.
cases.communicates: Structural interpretation. It helps relate annular high-curvature regions to portal-local geometry and semantic landmarks.

cases.title: Curvature plus collision radar
cases.description: Curvature heat map combined with collision radar labels for visible collision objects.
cases.communicates: Geometry-query interpretation. It shows where strong bending and visible collision structure do or do not line up.

cases.title: Full stack
cases.description: Reference Reality inset plus curvature, semantic glyphs, and collision radar overlays in one composed storytelling frame.
cases.communicates: Integrated interpretation. This is the most information-dense frame and closes the ladder by stacking perception, transport, and structure together.
